code:
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Immediate product failure (<1 month) or safety hazards
    - Final update status overrides initial positives ("UPDATE: Broke after...")
    - Clear negative declarations: "dangerous", "returned", "don't buy"

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failure despite partial utility
    - Persistent quality issues affecting daily use
    - Pattern: "Works BUT [critical flaw]..."

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with conditional utility ("good for guests")
    - Requires significant user effort for basic functionality
    - Temporary solution: "better than nothing"

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deduction for specific usability/non-core issues
    - Functional excellence with minor convenience drawbacks
    - Example: "Deducting one star for dishwasher safety but works perfectly"

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - "Still 5 stars" declarations override any deductions
    - Explicit overall ratings override aspect scores ("4 for comfort but 5 overall")
    - 3+ praised features outweighing single drawback
    - Minor critiques framed as afterthoughts to enthusiastic endorsement

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Safety issues →1 regardless of other positives
    - Final assessment rule: Last sentence sentiment dominates
    - Price praise + minor issues →5 if "worth every penny"
    - Core vs convenience: Functional defects →2-3, convenience →4
    - Multi-update reviews: Most recent evaluation prevails
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: UPDATE: Worked great initially but broke after 2 months. Not worth the price.
<Reasoning>Negative update overrides initial positive experience. Short lifespan →1.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - amazing features make this exceptional!"
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating override despite aspect score. Multi-feature praise confirms 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: Solves my jar problem perfectly! Deducting one star because handle isn't dishwasher safe.
<Reasoning>Functional excellence with specific convenience drawback. Usability impact warrants 4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: These towels look luxurious but need 3 washes to absorb. Deducting one star but still good post-break-in.
<Reasoning>Functional deficiency requiring user effort. Satisfied with reservations →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: Makes perfect smoothies but noisy enough to wake family. Deducting two stars.
<Reasoning>Core functionality excellent but major usability flaw. Mixed →3.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: Love this organizer! Tricky assembly but worth every penny for the storage space.
<Reasoning>Minor criticism preceded by strong endorsement. Transformative benefit →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: Sharp edges made assembly dangerous. Returned immediately despite good looks.
<Reasoning>Safety hazard overrides aesthetic positives. Clear 1-star.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: Blender overheats constantly. Smoothies great when it works, but unreliable.
<Reasoning>Core functionality compromised by reliability issues. Negative →2.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "This mattress gets 4 stars for comfort but there's so many features that deserve 5. Highly recommend!"
<Reasoning>Multi-feature praise overrides specific critique through strong recommendation.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
artifacts_json:

  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (30 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 12
  gold 5 -> 4: 9
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 1 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 1, 3] | Disagreement: 0.50
  2. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I would consider this 6 piece set to be hand towels. They are 16 inches by 30 inches. They are not the thinnest towels I have ever seen, and they are far from the plushest. I checked the description again and they have bath towels and wash cloths. Th..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 3, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I bought these mainly for sending leftovers. It is easier than sending my rubbermaid containers because I am not out much money if they don't come back."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Grunds bathmat is 100% certified organic cotton  with the main section being long straight cotton fibers and it has three stripes of loops on each end. This fiber is not treated with bleach, formaldehyde,  or any other harmful toxic substances. This ..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "The towels are definitely cute and well made. Very attractive. The only reason Im not giving them five stars is because they aren't very absorbent. Out of the package they aren't really absorbent at all. Throw them in the wash with some fabric soften..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "Ultra absorbant is definitely overselling it. They're reasonably nice towels and look nice without being particularly flashy or decorative. They do okay spot cleaning small messes and drying things up just a bit, but they're a long way from the most ..."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 3, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 30 errors, 17 borderline out of 80 examples."}
